title: The DeepMind trio who built a poker AI are now making money for quant hedge funds
slides: Three DeepMind poker AI builders now target hedge funds.
Their system exploits market patterns no human sees.
It does not trade like quant models you know.
Your fund's edge may already be outdated without it.
Ask your quant team to benchmark against their results.
thread_text:
1. Your edge is dying.

DeepMind's poker AI found market patterns no human can see. It's now targeting hedge funds.

2. Not quant.

This system doesn't use pricing models or mean reversion. It exploits game theory logic from the poker table.

3. Already live.

The builders left DeepMind to deploy it. Your competitors may be testing it now.

4. Blind spot.

Your quant models optimize for known signals. This one builds strategy from unseen opponent behavior.

5. One action today.

Ask your quant team to benchmark your fund's returns against their published results. If they can't, why not?

script: Poker AI now targets hedge funds.
It sees patterns no human can.
Your fund's edge may be outdated.
Ask your team to benchmark it.

instagram.caption:
Three former DeepMind researchers built a poker AI that beats pros, and now that same system is quietly managing real money at hedge funds. 🃏

Their approach doesn't scan for price patterns or buy low, sell high. It reads market moves like a poker opponent’s bluffs—extracting strategy from behavior no human trader notices. 📉🧠

Your fund's quant team may already be behind if they're not testing their results against it.

Ask your head of quant: “Can we replicate their published benchmarks, or are we flying blind?” 🔍

carousel_slides.description: DeepMind poker AI discovers market patterns no human can see, now targeting hedge funds.
No pricing models or mean reversion—it uses game theory logic from poker to exploit markets.
The builders left DeepMind to deploy it; rivals may be testing this unseen pattern system now.
Quant models optimize for known signals; this AI builds strategy from unseen opponent behavior.
Ask your quant team to benchmark your returns against their published results today.
